intro: Ericoides turrigerum, also known as Gynura turrigerum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of fever and cough. The bioactive constituents of Gynura turrigerum include flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gynura turrigerum is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
